title: Red Hat Security Advisory: galera and mariadb security update
overview:
An update for multiple packages is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux CodeReady Linux Builder (v. 9)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: galera and mariadb security update

An update for multiple packages is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for multiple packages is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

CVE-2023-52969: A flaw was found in MariaDB Server. This vulnerability may allow an attacker to cause a crash via an issue related to make_aggr_tables_info and optimize_stage2, resulting in an empty backtrace log.

CVE-2023-52970: A flaw was found in MariaDB Server. This vulnerability may allow an attacker to crash the database via Item_direct_view_ref::derived_field_transformer_for_where.

CVE-2025-21490: A flaw was found in the MySQL Server component: InnoDB. This vulnerability allows a high-privileged attacker to cause a denial of service, which causes frequent crashes or hangs, via multiple network protocols.

CVE-2025-30693: Vulnerability in the MySQL Server product of Oracle MySQL (component: InnoDB).  Supported versions that are affected are 8.0.0-8.0.41, 8.4.0-8.4.4 and  9.0.0-9.2.0. Easily exploitable vulnerability allows high privileged attacker with network access via multiple protocols to compromise MySQL Server.  Successful attacks of this vulnerability can result in unauthorized ability to cause a hang or frequently repeatable crash (complete
